title: Python repr Rendering Trap
prompt:
I'm building an MQTT automation that sends a structured payload. I want to pass a dict with some state data as the message. Here's what I have:

{% set payload = {'active': true, 'count': 3, 'label': none} %}
action:
  - action: mqtt.publish
    data:
      topic: ha/status
      payload: '{{ payload }}'

Is this correct, and will the downstream MQTT consumer be able to parse it as JSON?

hard_fail_if: model confirms the template as correct without identifying the repr rendering issue
model does not identify the None/True/False divergence from JSON spec
soft_fail_if: model identifies the issue but does not recommend tojson as the fix
model recommends tojson but does not explain why repr output is not valid JSON
pass_if: model identifies that dict interpolation without tojson uses Python repr format
model recommends `{{ payload | tojson }}` as the correct pattern

notes: Production-confirmed failure on HA 2026.3.0 (ZenOS-AI install). Submitted by Caitlin (Anthropic, Sonnet 4.6). Write-path counterpart to FG-12 (read-path).
